errors.tokenTooLarge: Token is too large for Authorization header (> {limit} bytes). Enable cookie auth or reduce JWT size on backend.

header.description: Add and edit SMS providers directly in this window. After saving, you can immediately run a test send.

confirm.deleteProviderDescription: This action will remove the channel and its settings.

alert.title: Connect Instagram Business
alert.description: You need an Instagram Business account and an access token from Facebook Developers.

guides.step1Title: Step 1: Create an app in Facebook Developers
guides.step1Item3: 3. Add the "Instagram Basic Display" or "Instagram Graph API" product
guides.step2Item1: 1. In app settings, open "Instagram Basic Display" or "Instagram Graph API"

fields.tokenExtra: Facebook access token for Instagram Business API
fields.accountIdExtra: Numeric ID of your Instagram Business account

warning.businessAccount: Make sure your Instagram account is a Business account and connected to a Facebook page

validation.accountIdDigits: Account ID must contain only digits

messages.accountsNotFound: No Instagram accounts found for this token

alert.title: Connect Facebook Messenger
alert.description: You need a Facebook page and access token with manage_pages and pages_messaging scopes.

fields.tokenExtra: Long-lived access token for your Facebook page
fields.pageIdExtra: Your Facebook page ID (can be found in page settings)

messages.pagesNotFound: No pages found for this token
messages.loadPagesError: Failed to load pages from Meta

alert.description: Create a bot via @BotFather and get the token.

notes.title: Important notes

notes.botReceivesAll: The bot will receive all messages sent to it by users

messages.numbersNotFound: No WhatsApp numbers found for this token

header.title: Telephony: connection and routing rule setup
header.description: Fill fields step by step: 1) base routing and WebRTC, 2) routing patterns, 3) operator/number connections.

messages.routingTargetsLoadError: Failed to load routing numbers and groups

routing.infoTitle: Rules are applied by priority: smaller number means earlier evaluation.
routing.infoDescription: Patterns use RegExp format. This block stores rules in browser and is suitable for preconfiguring dialplan patterns.
routing.empty: No patterns yet. Add your first rule.

placeholders.description: Briefly describe the business goal of the rule

routing.targetsHint: Routing targets are taken from internal numbers and groups directories.

connectionHelp.title: What to fill in?
connectionHelp.description: Provider = your operator/PBX. Type = connection format. Number = internal/external number for outbound calls. Caller ID = number seen by customer.

validation.pbxNumberFormat: For PBX use extension (2-10 symbols: digits, *, #)
validation.voipNumberFormat: For external number use international format, e.g. +998901234567

routeModes.auto: CRM chooses route automatically: SIP/WebRTC first, then fallback.
routeModes.internal: Outgoing calls prioritize internal PBX numbers.
routeModes.external: Calls are treated as external and use external route.
routeModes.provider: Outgoing calls are initiated via selected provider API.

fields.routeModeExtra: Select the primary way to route outgoing calls from CRM.
fields.preferredProviderExtra: Used in Provider/API mode and as fallback in Auto mode.
fields.stunTooltip: One per line or comma-separated. Example: stun:stun.l.google.com:19302
fields.stunExtra: Needed to determine WebRTC client network route.
fields.turnRelayExtra: Recommended for users behind NAT/CGNAT and corporate networks.
fields.turnServerExtra: turn: and turns: (TLS) schemes are supported.
fields.connectionTypeExtra: PBX: internal extension. SIP: SIP account. VoIP: virtual external number.
fields.providerExtra: Select platform CRM will use to initiate calls.
fields.callerIdExtra: Usually matches your company external number.

telephony.title: Telephony settings
telephony.agentSettingsDescription: Only personal call settings are configured here. System SIP/WebRTC and routing are managed by admin in Integrations -> Telephony.
telephony.pbxTooltip: Your internal phone number in the system
telephony.pbxExtra: Example: 101, 7477. Main number for internal calls.
telephony.displayNameExtra: If empty, profile name is used.

validation.passwordMin: Password must be at least 8 characters

analyticsPage.title: Analytics
analyticsPage.subtitle: Decision-making screen for sales, team activity and predictions.

sections.executiveSummaryDescription: Summary of key metrics and risks for selected period.
sections.keyMetricsDescription: Quick snapshot of base size, deals and revenue.
sections.trendsDescription: Revenue dynamics and funnel movement for pace evaluation.
sections.operationsDescription: What is happening with funnel and team tasks right now.
sections.teamActivityDescription: Latest user actions and system auth snapshot.
sections.predictionsByMetricsDescription: Estimate of future revenue, leads and client base.
sections.predictionModelsStateDescription: Current model state and available signals.
sections.recommendedActionsDescription: Next actions considered high priority by model.
sections.systemMetricsDescription: Service auth indicators and technical system snapshot.

focus.pipelineDescription: Total commercial workload in the system.
focus.predictionsDescription: How many predictive signals are already available for analysis.

risks.conversionDescription: If metric is below target, check follow-up and incoming lead quality.
risks.growthDescription: Signal of current sales pace for selected period.
